violations.text: At the time of inspection, TCS (Time/Temperature Control for Safety) Foods milk, nutrition shakes, and deli sandwiches were observed held at 49-50 degrees Fahrenheit in the 1st Floor Satellite Kitchen refrigerator. Food items were voluntarily disposed of by the PIC at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2025

The double glass doors down the hall from the kitchen area were observed to have a gap and not tightly sealing. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2025

The following equipment was observed in need of repair/adjustment: - The Air Gap drain at the kitchen prep sink was observed to be misaligned causing a leak onto the base of the cabinet the sink is set over. Air gap drain is to be adjusted to prevent spillage of liquid waste. - The 1st Floor Satellite Kitchen refrigerator was observed maintaining food temperatures at 49-50 degrees Fahrenheit. Unit was adjusted to a colder setting at the time of inspection. Maintenance staff were made aware of the observation of the refrigerator. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed to have an accumulation of residue. Non-food contact surfaces are to be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to preclude the buildup of soil and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2025

The ceiling to the kitchen area was observed to be a textured surface ("popcorn") and in need of resealing/repair (cracks, exposed joints). The ceiling is to be resealed/surfaced with a smooth, durable, easily cleanable material. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2026

The floor under the cookline area has an accumulation of soil residue and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/18/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with a buildup of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Unit was taken out of service and ice was disposed of at the time of inspection. Maintenance personnel began cleaning unit by end of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 09/09/2024
The tile grout in the kitchen area is in poor repair (worn/chipping/missing). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024

Yogurt and salad was observed held at 43-44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of foods. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Various foods were observed covered with plastic before properly cooling. All foods were recently prepared. Plastic cover was taken off of containers. PIC to go over proper cooling methods with employees. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023
The prep unit was unable to keep foods internal temperature below 41 degrees. Unit to be used for non TCS foods only until unit is serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023
